summary: Multi-modal training has 4 real issues and 1 fake finding.

findings.title: Cross-attention image K,V projections recomputed every call
evidence_refs: nsys: repeated linear projection kernels in fusion forward
findings.metric: ~30% of fusion time in redundant projections
findings.description: CrossModalFusion.forward() recomputes image K and V projections on every call. For fixed image features (common in multi-modal training), these should be pre-computed and cached.
findings.suggestion: Pre-compute image K,V in a separate method and pass cached values to forward.

findings.title: Text and image encoders run sequentially instead of parallel
evidence_refs: nsys: sequential encoder execution, no stream overlap
findings.description: MultiModalModel.forward() runs text_encoder and image_encoder sequentially. Since they operate on independent inputs, they could run in parallel using separate CUDA streams.
findings.suggestion: Use torch.cuda.Stream to run text and image encoders in parallel, then synchronize before fusion.

findings.title: Use inference_mode instead of no_grad in evaluation
evidence_refs: nsys: version counter atomic ops in eval forward
findings.description: MultiModalTrainingLoop.evaluate() uses @torch.no_grad() decorator. torch.inference_mode() provides additional speedup by disabling version counter tracking.

findings.title: Redundant forward pass in eval — computes unused logits
evidence_refs: nsys: text_pool and image_pool computation visible in eval
findings.description: evaluate() calls model.forward() which computes text_logits, image_logits, and fusion_logits, but only fusion_logits is used for accuracy. The text and image logit computations are wasted.
findings.suggestion: Add an eval mode flag to MultiModalModel that skips unused logit computations.

findings.title: Eval metrics should be computed on GPU instead of CPU
evidence_refs: nsys: cudaMemcpy D2H in metric computation
findings.metric: negligible — metrics are already on GPU
findings.description: EvalMetrics.compute_accuracy() calls .item() which transfers to CPU. The metrics should stay on GPU for batch accumulation.
findings.suggestion: Accumulate metrics on GPU and only transfer to CPU at the end of evaluation.
